commands.review_summary:
The PR expands the bundled Webhooks plugin with authenticated generic HTTP ingress, Gateway-managed dynamic subscriptions and CLI commands, agent/TaskFlow/delivery dispatch, completion delivery, docs/tests, and a trusted-official-install scheduled-turn hook change.

PR surface: Source +3215, Tests +2594, Docs +1172. Total +6981 across 30 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: the problematic paths are source-reproducible from the merge ref without running tests. I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers include security/API contract decisions and real-environment proof, not only mechanical code repairs.

target.title: Add generic webhooks plugin
target.body:
## Summary

- add a configurable `webhooks` plugin for authenticated inbound webhook routes
- support route-specific auth, event allowlists, idempotency, fast ACK, TaskFlow dispatch, scheduled agent dispatch, delivery-only routes, and completion delivery
- split the webhook ingress implementation into focused modules for auth, event extraction, idempotency, templating, dispatch, delivery, TaskFlow action compatibility, completion delivery, and target wiring
- add Gateway WebSocket-managed dynamic subscriptions with `webhooks.subscribe`, `webhooks.list`, `webhooks.remove`, and `webhooks.test`
- add `openclaw webhook subscribe/list/remove/test` CLI wiring that calls the Gateway RPC surface over OpenClaw's existing WebSocket control plane
- keep external provider delivery on standard HTTP webhook POST routes, matching the Hermes-style data-plane model instead of introducing a separate local relay connector
- remove the standalone WebSocket relay connector and its `ws` dependency from the webhooks plugin

## Verification

- `PATH=/Users/bytedance/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH node scripts/test-extension.mjs webhooks`
  - 3 files passed, 50 tests passed
- `git diff --check`
- attempted full-repo `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project tsconfig.json --incremental false`
  - failed from TypeScript/Node heap OOM at both default heap and `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`; no TypeScript diagnostics were produced before OOM
- attempted extension-level `tsc` probe using `extensions/voice-call/tsconfig.json`
  - not a valid plugin-local check in this repo shape; it fails on existing workspace path resolution for `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Enterprise systems can post authenticated webhook events into OpenClaw and trigger TaskFlows, scheduled agent runs, or delivery actions without hard-coding a specific provider. Dynamic route management now goes through OpenClaw Gateway RPC, so CLI/operator control uses the existing Gateway WebSocket plane.

Real environment previously tested for direct HTTP ingress: Local OpenClaw gateway with the webhooks plugin installed, real Codebase webhook delivery, and a real Codebase merge request in `iaasng/openclaw-session-search`.

Exact steps previously run for the Codebase direct-ingress proof: Configured a Codebase `merge_request` webhook to `/plugins/webhooks/codebase-mr-review`, pushed marker commits to MR `!9`, observed `202 Accepted` deliveries, watched OpenClaw schedule and run the webhook reviewer agent, and confirmed the MR received an automatic review note.

Evidence from the direct-ingress proof: Codebase delivery `776811841624662` reached OpenClaw; logs showed `[webhooks] agent dispatch scheduled`, `[webhooks] exec delivery completed`, and `[webhooks] delivered agent completion`; MR `!9` received automatic review note `776811996803384`.

Observed result from the direct-ingress proof: The webhook request returned quickly, duplicate/event-filter paths remained guarded, the agent reviewed the real MR diff, and completion delivery wrote the review back to Codebase.

Gateway WebSocket proof added in this patch: Unit/integration-level plugin coverage registers `webhooks.subscribe` through the Gateway RPC surface, persists a dynamic GitHub-style subscription without restarting the plugin, then dispatches a signed HTTP webhook through the prefix route and verifies scheduled agent dispatch. This proves OpenClaw's existing WebSocket control plane manages webhook subscriptions while the provider data plane remains standard HTTP webhook ingress.

Not yet completed: The stricter acceptance bar still needs fresh real E2E reports for GitHub PR, Codebase MR, and Meego requirement-created events on a reachable OpenClaw instance. Do not treat this PR as fully production-validated for all three providers until those reports are attached.
target.author: marchpure
